_note: Refreshed by the refresh-sports-feed scheduled task. Each item is {headline, url, source, published (ISO8601), snark}. Snark voice: Barstool-adjacent guy-at-the-bar, crass, horny, cocky, zero filler. One sentence. No slurs, no punching down at specific real people in a harmful way. Punch sideways (at media, front offices, rival fanbases) and up (at ownership). Keep it Boston.

items.headline: Contreras suspended 7 games for scuffle, appeals and plays Friday
items.snark: Contreras tapping his helmet got him ejected, MLB umps softer than the infield grass at Tropicana.

items.headline: Chapman breaks MLB reliever strikeout record with 1,364th K
items.snark: Chapman now owns more K's than a Taylor Swift concert, still can't fix this rotation's ERA though.

items.headline: Suarez, Chapman named All-Stars; Gray, Rafaela, Contreras snubbed
items.snark: Last place teams sending two All-Stars is like your unemployed cousin showing up to Thanksgiving in a Lambo, we have questions.

items.headline: Vrabel and wife Jen spotted heading to Taylor Swift-Kelce wedding
items.snark: Vrabel at the Swift-Kelce wedding means we're officially Hollywood now, can't wait for the Netflix doc.

items.headline: Mike Vrabel, wife Jen spotted heading to Taylor Swift-Kelce wedding
items.snark: Vrabel in a tux at MSG for Swelce nuptials, the most important Patriots event happening this Fourth of July.

items.headline: 25 thoughts on state of Patriots after minicamp and OTAs
items.snark: 25 thoughts is 24 more than anyone had watching our special teams last year, good luck with that.

items.headline: Patriots coach Jeremy Springer engaged to NBC Boston host Kayla Burton
items.snark: Springer proposing is the only big move the Patriots made this week, congrats to the happy couple.
